attributes.@qa08: Correctness ✓ (R2=5×3=15, R3=6×4=24, R4=7×4=28, R5=9×5=45 — all correct). Skill alignment ✓ (multiplicative, not additive). Randomization: no randomizer; static content covers ×5–×9 range ✓. Hints: wrongHint + wrongHint2 now both say 'Think × not +' targeting M1 — ✓. Age band ✓ Grade 3–5. WARN: no time pressure enforced at difficulty 3+ (spec requires ≤4s window for ×6–×9). Gap is architectural — untimed by P1 design.
attributes.@qa09: WARN (not blocking): Add soft time pressure at multiply block rounds 3–5 in a future P2 pass. Current static content is mathematically correct and hint quality is good.

attributes.@qa08: All division prompts audited: zero occurrences of 'take away', 'remove', 'subtract' in division context. R6: 'how many stations can the fleet cover?' ✓. R7: 'how many piers can the fleet reach?' ✓. R8: 'how many full units can you cover?' ✓. R9: 'how many substations get full coverage?' ✓. Division equation reveal now shows totalPool ÷ trucksPerUnit = answer ✓. WrongHint2 added for all divide rounds using multiplicative inverse framing ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — no fixes needed. Division-as-partitioning language is consistent across all 4 divide rounds.

attributes.@qa08: R10 (4×9=36) and R11 (8×6=48) are tagged multi-step. Final answers are correct. WrongHint + wrongHint2 both scaffold the two-step process (multiply first, then check fleet). Step label added to HUD showing 'Step 1: multiply · Step 2: verify fleet'. WARN: no locked step-by-step UI — single input accepts final answer. Spec calls for greyed Step 2 field until Step 1 solved. This is a P2 architectural item.
attributes.@qa09: WARN (not blocking for P1): Step-by-step locked UI deferred to P2. Current hints adequately scaffold the two steps via wrongHint/wrongHint2 text.

attributes.@qa08: CRITICAL BUG FIXED (2026-04-09): R8 was 20÷4=5 (exact — no remainder, M3 trap impossible). R9 was 30÷5=6 (exact — no remainder). Both were answering wrong values. Fixed: R8 now 22÷4=5 r2 (answer=5 full units, 2 trucks in reserve). R9 now 27÷5=5 r2 (answer=5 substations). Division equation reveal now shows remainder ✓. WrongHint2 explicitly names the remainder ✓. WARN: no leftover-firefighter animation (requires sprite layer — P2 item).
attributes.@qa09: M3 remainder trap now mathematically valid. Data corrected. Animation deferred to P2.

attributes.@qa08: R0 (2×3=6) and R1 (4×2=8) are intro rounds. On success, equation reveal shows trucksPerUnit × units = answer and labels it 'Used multiplication — each unit needs its own full crew' ✓. This names the operation explicitly as required. WrongHint scaffolds the multiplication concept without revealing answer ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — intro rounds correctly introduce multiplication and name the operation on success.

attributes.@qa08: Multiply block covers ×5 (R2), ×6 (R3), ×7 (R4), ×9 (R5). ×2, ×3, ×4 appear only in intro (R0, R1). Spec says 4-round session must guarantee factors 2, 3, 4 each appear at least once. Static content partially covers but no randomizer exists. WARN: no randomizer — content is static by P1 design.
attributes.@qa09: WARN: randomizer deferred to P2. Static content is pedagogically reasonable for P1 demonstration.

attributes.@qa08: Multiply block has ×6 (R3) and ×7 (R4) but no ×8. Spec requires at least 2 from {×6,×7,×8}. Has 2/3 ✓. No randomizer — static. WARN: ×8 not covered. No randomizer.
attributes.@qa09: WARN: add a ×8 round to the multiply block in P2 content expansion. Randomizer deferred to P2.

attributes.@qa08: R6 (18÷3=6) and R7 (28÷4=7). Both exact quotients (no remainder) ✓. Prompts use 'per station'/'per pier' — multiplicative language, no subtraction ✓. Division equation reveal shows totalPool ÷ trucksPerUnit = answer ✓. WrongHint2 reframes as inverse multiplication ('what number times 4 equals 28?') ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — exact dividends, correct partitioning language, good hint quality.

attributes.@qa08: R8 (22÷4=5 r2) and R9 (27÷5=5 r2). FIXED: numbers now non-divisible so M3 remainder trap is real. Prompts frame it as 'leftover trucks stay in reserve' ✓. Answer parser accepts 5 (floor), not 5.5 or 6 ✓. Equation reveal shows 22÷4=5 r2 and 27÷5=5 r2 ✓. WrongHint2 explicitly states the remainder value ('2 left over') without revealing the answer ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — M3 trap is now mathematically valid. Remainder blindness is correctly targeted.

attributes.@qa09: WARN (P2 item): two-step locked UI not implemented. Acceptable for P1 — text scaffolding covers the gap.

attributes.@qa08: Same as FD-M6: no locked two-step UI. Remainder scenario in M7 is now correctly implemented in R8/R9 (non-divisible numbers, M3 trap valid). Progressive hints now show wrongHint2 after 2nd wrong attempt ✓. Hint progression unlocks in correct order ✓.
attributes.@qa09: WARN (P2 item): two-step locked UI deferred. Hint progression now correctly implemented.

attributes.@qa08: R11 (Skyline Tower, 8×6=48) is now marked isBossRound=true. Hint display is suppressed — wrong-attempt overlay shows 'Boss round · no hints' and 'Use your skills — no hints on the final zone' ✓. No blocking mechanism for T1–T4 PASS requirement — all prior rounds must be completed in sequence naturally ✓.
attributes.@qa09: WARN: no explicit 'T1–T4 must pass before boss' gate — game progresses linearly so all prior rounds are always completed first. Blocking is structural by default.

attributes.@qa08: No time pressure at any difficulty level. All multiplication rounds are untimed — player can compute for unlimited seconds before dispatching. Spec requires ≤4s response window for ×6–×9 at difficulty 3+. This is an architectural gap requiring a timer component. WARN not FAIL for P1: time pressure is a P2+ feature.
attributes.@qa09: WARN (P2 item): add soft time pressure (countdown bar, not punitive) for multiply block rounds at higher difficulty. Observer protocol latency measurement is not yet in place.

attributes.@qa08: Full audit of all game text strings completed 2026-04-09. Zero occurrences of 'remove', 'take away', 'subtract' in any division-context prompt or hint. All 4 divide rounds (R6–R9) use partitioning language: 'how many groups', 'how many stations can the fleet cover', 'leftover trucks stay in reserve' ✓. Introduction screen uses 'drain the fleet' in a general context (not division-specific) — acceptable ✓.
attributes.@qa09: PASS — subtraction-division confusion is fully mitigated across all text strings.
